{{short description|Men's football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Tanzania women's national football team}}
{{Use British English|date = October 2014}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=October 2014}}
{{Infobox National football team
| Name              = Tanzania| Nickname          = ''The Taifa Stars''
| Badge             = 
| Association       = [[Tanzania Football Federation]] (TFF)
| Sub-confederation = [[Council for East and Central Africa Football Associations|CECAFA]] (East & Central Africa)
| Confederation     = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach             = [[Miguel Ángel Gamondi]] 
| Most caps         = [[Erasto Nyoni]] (107)
| Top scorer        = [[Simon Msuva]] & [[Mrisho Ngasa]] (25)
| Home Stadium      = [[Benjamin Mkapa Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme    = TAN
| FIFA Rank         = {{FIFA World Rankings|TAN}}
| FIFA max          = 65
| FIFA max date     = February 1995
| FIFA min          = 175
| FIFA min date     = October–November 2005
| Elo Rank          = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Tanzania}}
| Elo max           = 75
| Elo max date      = 11 November 1979
| Elo min           = 168
| Elo min date      = 19 December 2004

| pattern_la1 =_tan24h
| pattern_b1  =_tan24h
| pattern_ra1 =_tan24h
| leftarm1    =0000FF
| body1       =0000FF
| rightarm1   =0000FF
| shorts1     =0000FF
| socks1      =0000FF
| pattern_la2 =_yellowborder
| pattern_b2  =_tan24a
| pattern_ra2 =_yellowborder
| pattern_sh2 =_shorts
| leftarm2    =FFFF00
| body2       =FFFF00
| rightarm2   =FFFF00
| shorts2     =0000FF
| socks2      =0000FF
| pattern_la3 =_tan24t
| pattern_b3  =_tan24t
| pattern_ra3 =_tan24t
| leftarm3    =000000
| body3       =000000
| rightarm3   =000000
| shorts3     =000000
| socks3      =000000

| First game         = {{fb|UGA|colonial}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|Tanganyika}}<br>(Uganda; Date Unknown 1945)<br>''as '''Tanzania'''''<br>{{fb|TAN}} 1–1 {{fb-rt|KEN}}<br>(Tanzania; 1 March 1969)
| Largest win        = {{fb|TAN}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|SOM}}<br>([[Jinja, Uganda|Jinja]], Uganda; 1 December 1995)<br>{{fb|SOM}} 0–7 {{fb-rt|TAN}}<br>([[Kampala]], Uganda; 1 December 2012)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|Tanganyika}} 0–9 {{fb-rt|KEN|colonial}}<br>([[Tanganyika (1961–1964)|Tanganyika]]; Date Unknown 1956)<br>''as '''Tanzania'''''<br>{{fb|KSA}} 8–0 {{fb-rt|TAN}}<br>([[Dammam]], Saudi Arabia; 11 September 1998)
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 5
| Regional cup first = [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
| Regional cup best  = Round of 16 ([[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]])
|Captain=[[Mbwana Samatta]]|2ndRegional cup apps=3|2ndRegional name=[[African Nations Championship]]|2ndRegional cup first=[[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]|2ndRegional cup best=Group stage ([[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]], [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]])|3rdRegional cup apps=3|3rdRegional name=[[COSAFA Cup]]|3rdRegional cup first=[[1997 COSAFA Cup|1997]]|3rdRegional cup best=Third place ([[2017 COSAFA Cup|2017]])|4thRegional cup apps=36|4thRegional name=[[CECAFA Cup]]|4thRegional cup first=[[1973 CECAFA Cup|1973]]|4thRegional cup best='''Champions''' ([[1974 CECAFA Cup|1974]], [[1994 CECAFA Cup|1994]], [[2010 CECAFA Cup|2010]])}}

The '''Tanzania national football team''' ({{langx|sw|Timu ya Taifa ya Mpira wa Miguu ya Tanzania}}) represents [[Tanzania]] in men's international [[association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Tanzania Football Federation]], the governing body for [[football in Tanzania]], Tanzania's home ground is [[Benjamin Mkapa National Stadium]] in [[Dar es Salaam]] and their head coach is [[Hemed Morocco]] from Tanzania. They are colloquially known as the ''Taifa Stars''. Tanzania has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]]. Before uniting with Zanzibar, the team played as the '''Tanganyika national football team'''. The team represents both [[FIFA]] and [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF).

The island of [[Zanzibar]], part of Tanzania (and once an independent nation), is also an associate member of CAF and has played matches with other nations, but is not eligible to enter the World Cup or [[Africa Cup of Nations]]. See ''[[Zanzibar national football team]]''.

==History==
Since qualifying for the [[1980 Africa Cup of Nations]], Tanzania endured nearly 40 years without major success, struggling in both African and World Cup qualifiers. Their best effort was in [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]], where the ''Taifa Stars'' defeated [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]] twice and only finished three points behind group winners [[Senegal national football team|Senegal]]. In 2010 Tanzania won the [[CECAFA Cup]] for the third time.{{citation needed|date=April 2021}} 

A recent achievement was a 1–0 win against [[South Africa national football team|South Africa]] in the [[2017 COSAFA Cup]] [[2017 COSAFA Cup#Quarter-finals|Quarter-finals]]. But afterwards, Tanzania lost the Semi-finals 2–4 to [[Zambia national football team|Zambia]]. Then, in the Third-place playoff, Tanzania managed to win the match against [[Lesotho national football team|Lesotho]] 4–2 in a [[Penalty shoot-out (association football)|penalty shootout]] after the [[Overtime (sports)|extra time]] ended in a goalless draw. This Third place was considered Tanzania's biggest football achievement in many years.{{citation needed|date=April 2021}}

On 24 March 2019, Tanzania beat East African rivals [[Uganda national football team|Uganda]] 3–0 to reach the finals of the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] for the first time in 39 years. At the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|Finals]] the ''Taifa Stars'', being the weakest team in the group, lost all three Group C matches, as predicted. A few months later, Tanzania qualified for the [[African Nations Championship]] for only the second time, as well as defeating [[Burundi national football team|Burundi]] in the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round|2022 World Cup qualifiers]].{{citation needed|date=April 2021}} 

On 19 November 2024 Tanzania won a narrow victory of 1–0 against Guinea which secured their fourth times [https://www.thecitizen.co.tz/tanzania/sports/tanzania-reach-afcon-finals-for-fourth-time-4829228 Afcon qualification] since 1980. A decisive goal by Simon Msuva sent them to the tournament in 2025. The Afcon finals are scheduled to take place from 21 December 2025 to 18 January 2026 in Morocco.

In February 2026, [[Ministry of Information, Culture, Arts and Sports (Tanzania)|Tanzania's Ministry of Sports]] extended [[Miguel Ángel Gamondi]]'s contract to coach the national football team.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Africa |first=Sport News |title=Tanzania: Miguel Gamondi will extend as coach - Brève Sport News Africa |url=https://sportnewsafrica.com/en/alerts/tanzania-miguel-gamondi-will-extend-as-coach |access-date=2026-02-16 |website=Sport News Africa |language=en}}</ref>

==Team image==
=== Kit suppliers ===
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Kit supplier
! Period
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[Uhlsport]]
| 2017–2023
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} Sandaland
| 2023–
|-
|}

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Tanzania national football team results}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
| date       = 5 September
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| team1      = {{fb-rt|CGO}} 
| team2      = {{fb|TAN}} 
| score      = 1–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020792
| goals1     =
*[[Dechan Moussavou|Moussavou]] {{goal|68}}
| goals2     =
*[[Selemani Mwalimu|Mwalimu]] {{goal|84}}
| stadium    = [[Stade Alphonse Massemba-Débat]]
| location   = [[Brazzaville]], Congo
| attendance =
| result     = D
| referee    = Thabang Ketshabile ([[Botswana Football Association|Botswana]])
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
| date       = 9 September
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|3}}
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}} 
| team2      = {{fb|NIG}} 
| score      = 0–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020791
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
*[[Daniel Sosah|Sosah]] {{goal|58}}
| stadium    = [[Amaan Stadium]]
| location   = [[Zanzibar City]], Tanzania
| attendance =
| result     = L
| referee    = [[Mustapha Ghorbal]] ([[Algerian Football Federation|Algeria]])
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
| date       = 8 October
| time       = {{UTZ|22:00|3}}
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 World Cup qualification]]
| team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}} 
| team2      = {{fb|ZAM}} 
| score      = 0–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021056
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
*[[Fashion Sakala|F. Sakala]] {{goal|75}}
| stadium    = [[Amaan Stadium]]
| location   = [[Zanzibar City]], Tanzania
| attendance =
| result     = L
| referee    = Ibrahim Kalilou Traore ([[Ivorian Football Federation|Ivory Coast]])
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 14 October
| time       = {{UTZ|20:00|+4}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|IRN}}
| score      = 2–0
| team2      = {{fb|TAN}}
| report     = https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/759665/tanzania-iran
| goals1     =
*[[Amirhossein Hosseinzadeh|Hosseinzadeh]] {{goal|17|pen}}
*[[Mohammad Mohebi|Mohebi]] {{goal|26}}
| goals2     =
| location   = [[Dubai]], United Arab Emirates
| stadium    = [[Rashid Stadium]]
| attendance = 
| referee    = Adel Al Naqbi ([[United Arab Emirates Football Association|United Arab Emirates]])
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|date= 15 November
|time= {{UTZ|18:00|2}}
|round= [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|score= 4–3
|report= https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/cesdwwnxbc5fmajgroc0hqzy2/8pw8cm9fuczk7lmxm1oxjdyc4/1rj1740ys47h5s938mfswsh78/7w7yepmmwhmq672dm8t1a8h04?date=2025-11-15
|team1= {{fb-rt|KUW}}
|goals1=
|team2= {{fb|TAN}}
|goals2=
|stadium= [[Al Salam Stadium (Egypt)|Al Salam Stadium]]
|location= [[Cairo]], Egypt
|attendance=
|referee=
|format= 1
|result= L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025 AFCON GS]]
|date       = 23 December
|time       = {{UTZ|18:30|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|NGA}}
|score      = 2–1
|team2      = {{fb|TAN}}
|goals1     = *[[Semi Ajayi|Ajayi]] {{goal|36}}
*[[Ademola Lookman|Lookman]] {{goal|52}}
|goals2     = [[Charles M'Mombwa|M'Mombwa]] {{goal|50}}
|stadium    = [[Fez Stadium]]
|location   = [[Fez, Morocco|Fez]], Morocco
|attendance = 11,444
|referee    = [[Dahane Beida]] ([[Football Federation of the Islamic Republic of Mauritania|Mauritania]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506778
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025 AFCON GS]]
|date       = 27 December
|time       = {{UTZ|18:30|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|UGA}}
|score      = 1–1
|team2      = {{fb|TAN}}
|goals1     = [[Uche Ikpeazu|Ikpeazu]] {{goal|80}}
|goals2     = [[Simon Msuva|Msuva]] {{goal|59|pen}}
|stadium    = [[Al Medina Stadium]]
|location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance = 10,540
|referee    = [[Jalal Jayed]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506791
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025 AFCON GS]]
|date       = 30 December
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
|score      = 1–1
|team2      = {{fb|TUN}}
|goals1     = [[Feisal Salum|Salum]] {{goal|48}}
|goals2     = [[Ismaël Gharbi|Gharbi]] {{goal|43|pen}}
|stadium    = [[Rabat Olympic Stadium]]
|location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance = 15,205
|referee    = [[Jean-Jacques Ndala]] ([[Congolese Association Football Federation|DR Congo]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506803
|result     = D
}}

===2026=== 
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025 AFCON R16]]
|date       = 4 January
|time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MAR}}
|score      = 1–0
|team2      = {{fb|TAN}}
|goals1     = [[Brahim Díaz|Díaz]] {{goal|64}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Prince Moulay Abdellah Stadium]]
|location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance = 63,894
|referee    = Boubou Traore ([[Malian Football Federation|Mali]])
|report     = https://www.cafonline.com/afcon2025/match-centre?competition=190&season=2025&match=2506812
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 26 March
| time       = {{UTZ|16:30|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
| score      = 0–1
| team2      = {{fb|LIE}}
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292887/292888/400021870?date=2026-03-26
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = [[Ferhat Sağlam|Sağlam]] {{goal|55}}
| location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
| stadium    = [[Kigali Pelé Stadium]]
| attendance =
| referee    = [[Mohamed Diraneh]] ([[Djiboutian Football Federation|Djibouti]])
| result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 29 March
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|MAC}}
| score      = 0–6
| team2      = {{fb|TAN}}
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292887/292888/400021872?date=2026-03-29
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = *Amâncio {{goal|16|o.g.}}
*[[Bakari Mwamnyeto|Mwamnyeto]] {{goal|26}}
*[[Mudathir Yahya|Yahya]] {{goal|45}}
*[[Paul Peter|Peter]] {{goal|56}}
*[[Novatus Miroshi|Miroshi]] {{goal|74}}
*[[Tarryn Allarakhia|Allarakhia]] {{goal|87}}
| location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
| stadium    = [[Kigali Pelé Stadium]]
| attendance = 12,900
| referee    = [[Lucky Kasalirwe]] ([[Federation of Uganda Football Associations|Uganda]])
| result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 4 June
| time       = {{UTZ|21:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
| score      = Cancelled
| team2      = {{fb|UGA}}
| report     = 
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Marrakesh Stadium]]
| location   = [[Marrakesh]], Morocco
| referee    = 
| result     = v
}}
{{Football box collapsible
| format     = 1
| round      = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
| date       = 8 June
| time       = {{UTZ|21:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
| score      = Cancelled
| team2      = {{fb|RWA}}
| report     = 
| goals1     =
| goals2     =
| stadium    = [[Marrakesh Stadium]]
| location   = [[Marrakesh]], Morocco
| referee    = 
| result     = v
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|MAD}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Tanzania
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|MAD}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TAN}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Madagascar
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|NGA}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TAN}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Nigeria
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|NGA}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Tanzania
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TAN}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|GNB}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Tanzania
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|GNB}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TAN}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Guinea-Bissau
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaching staff==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
|Head coach|| {{flagicon|ARG}} [[Miguel Ángel Gamondi]] 
|-
|Assistant coach|| {{flagicon|TAN}} [[Juma Mgunda]] 
|-
|Assistant coach|| {{flagicon|TAN}} [[Hemed Morocco]]
|-
|Goalkeeper coach|| {{flagicon|KSA}} [[Osama Al-Hamdan]]
|-
|Physical coach|| {{flagicon|FIN}} [[Mikael Mayoyo]]
|-
|Sports therapist|| {{flagicon|BEL}} [[Joris De Vos]]
|-
|Head of medical|| {{flagicon|TAN}} [[Lisobine Kisongo]]
|-
|Team manager|| {{flagicon|TAN}} Reinhard
|-
| Video analysis|| {{flagicon|TAN}} [[Gabriel Johnson (footballer)|Gabriel Johnson]]
|}

===Coaching history===

:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.
{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Bert Trautmann]] (1975)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Geoff Hudson]] (1977–1979)
*{{flagicon|POL|1928}} [[Sławomir Wolk]] (1979–1980)<ref>{{cite web |url=http://brotherdanny5b.blogspot.com/2013/10/taifa-stars-ilivyofuzu-kwenda-lagos-1980.html |title=Brother Danny: Taifa Stars Ilivyofuzu Kwenda Lagos 1980 |website=brotherdanny5b.blogspot.com |access-date=15 January 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171210143627/http://brotherdanny5b.blogspot.com/2013/10/taifa-stars-ilivyofuzu-kwenda-lagos-1980.html |archive-date=10 December 2017 |url-status=dead}}</ref>
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Mahammed Msomali]] (1980–1981)
*{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Rudi Gutendorf]] (1981)
*{{flagicon|ZAN}} Joseph Bendera (1981–1985)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1987af.html|title=International Matches 1987 - Africa|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=7 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221207044052/https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1987af.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
*{{flagicon|TAN}}  Paul West (1985–1989)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1992af.html|title=International Matches 1992 - Africa|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=28 November 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221128110031/https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1992af.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Charles Boniface Mkwasa]] (1989–1992)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Sunday Kayuni]] (1993–1995)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1993af.html|title=International Matches 1993 - Africa|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=3 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221203024528/https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1993af.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Clóvis de Oliveira]] (1995–1996)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Badru Hafidh]] (1996–1998)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} Sylersaid Mziray (1998–1999)<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1998af.html|title=International Matches 1998 - Africa|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=7 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221207032356/https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1998af.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Mansour Magram]] (1999–00)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Burkhard Pape]] (2000–01)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Mshindo Msolla]] (2001–02)
*{{flagicon|KEN}} [[James Siang'a]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Mshindo Msolla]] (2002–03)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Badru Hafidh]] (2003–06)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Júlio César Leal]] (2006)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Márcio Máximo]] (2006–10)
*{{flagicon|DEN}} [[Jan B. Poulsen|Jan Poulsen]] (2010–12)
*{{flagicon|DEN}} [[Kim Poulsen]] (2012–14)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} ''[[Salum Madadi]]'' (2014)
*{{flagicon|NED}} [[Mart Nooij]] (2014–2015)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Charles Boniface Mkwasa]] (2015–2017)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} [[Salum Mayanga]] (2017–2018)
*{{flagicon|NGA}} [[Emmanuel Amunike]] (2018–2019)
*{{flagicon|BDI}} [[Etienne Ndayiragije]] (2019–2021)
*{{flagicon|DEN}} [[Kim Poulsen]] (2021–2022)
*{{flagicon|ZAM}} ''[[Honour Janza]]'' (2022)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} ''[[Dean Alty]]'' (2022)
*{{flagicon|DEN}} [[Kim Poulsen]] (2022–2023)
*{{flagicon|ALG}} [[Adel Amrouche]] (2023–2024)
*{{flagicon|TAN}} ''[[Hemed Morocco]]'' (2024–2025)
*{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Miguel Ángel Gamondi]] (2025–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)]] matches against [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]] and [[Macau national football team|Macau]] on 26 and 29 March 2026, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.tff.or.tz/gamondi-aita-25-stars/|title=Gamondi aita 25 Stars|trans-title=Gamondi calls up 25 players|publisher=[[Tanzania Football Federation]]|date=18 March 2026|language=sw}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 29 March 2026, after the match against [[Macau national football team|Macau]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=Yonna Geoffrey|age={{birth date and age|1999|08|09|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Pamba S.C.|Pamba]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=GK|name=Zuberi Foba|age={{birth date and age|2002|5|23|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=GK|name=[[Aishi Manula]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|9|13|df=yes}}|caps=66|goals=0|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Yona Amosi|age={{birth date and age|1999|8|9|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Pamba S.C.|Pamba]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=MF|name=[[Haji Mnoga]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2002|4|16}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[Salford City F.C.|Salford City]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Nickson Kibabage]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|10|12|df=yes}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=DF|name=Lameck Lawi|age={{birth date and age|2005|9|12|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=Nuru Twalib|age={{birth date and age|2003|1|1|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=DF|name=[[Bakari Mwamnyeto]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|vice-captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1995|10|5}}|caps=54|goals=1|club=[[Young Africans S.C.|Young Africans]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=DF|name=[[Mohamed Husseini]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|11|1|df=y}}|caps=65|goals=1|club=[[Young Africans S.C.|Young Africans]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=24|pos=DF|name=Mohamed Mussa|age={{birth date and age|2004|04|29|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=Mashujaa|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ibrahim Hamad (footballer)|Ibrahim Hamad]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|11|12|df=y}}|caps=37|goals=1|club=[[Young Africans S.C.|Young Africans]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=MF|name=[[Feisal Salum]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1998|1|11}}|caps=62|goals=5|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Charles M'Mombwa]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1998|3|14}}|caps=20|goals=4|club=[[Floriana FC|Floriana]]|clubnat=MLT}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=MF|name=[[Morice Abraham]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|8|13|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=MF|name=Kelvin Nashon|age={{birth date and age|2000|08|02|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Pamba S.C.|Pamba]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=MF|name=Alphonce Msanga|age={{birth date and age|2003|6|14|df=yes}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Şamaxı FK|Şamaxı]]|clubnat=AZE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=MF|name=[[Novatus Miroshi]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2002|09|02}}|caps=37|goals=4|club=[[Göztepe S.K.|Göztepe]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=MF|name=[[Mudathir Yahya]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1995|5|6}}|caps=41|goals=2|club=[[Young Africans S.C.|Young Africans]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=Yusuf Kagoma|age={{birth date and age|1996|4|3|df=yes}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[Iddy Nado]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|11|3|df=yes}}|caps=25|goals=1|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=Paul Peter|age={{birth date and age|2003|11|30|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=1|club=[[JKT Tanzania]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=FW|name=[[Kelvin John]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|6|10|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=1|club=[[AaB Fodbold|AaB]]|clubnat=DEN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Tarryn Allarakhia]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1997|11|17}}|caps=14|goals=2|club=[[Rochdale A.F.C.|Rochdale]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=[[Selemani Mwalimu]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|1|19|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=22|pos=FW|name=Bakari Msimu|age={{birth date and age|2007|12|10|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Coastal Union F.C.|Coastal Union]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Simon Msuva]]|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|1993|10|2}}|caps=103|goals=25|club=[[Al-Talaba SC|Al-Talaba]]|clubnat=IRQ}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Tanzania in the last 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=1|pos=GK|name=Hussein Masalanga|age={{birth date and age|1992|03|04|df=yes}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Singida Black Stars FC|Singida Black Stars]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=13|pos=GK|name=Yakoub Suleiman Ali|age={{birth date and age|1999|12|07|df=yes}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=Ally Salim|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2000|4|19}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|MAR}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=2|pos=DF|name=Pascal Msindo|age={{birth date and age|2003|8|15|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Dickson Job]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|12|29|df=y}}|caps=43|goals=1|club=[[Young Africans S.C.|Young Africans]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=16|pos=DF|name=Wilson Nangu|age={{birth date and age|2001|09|30|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=19|pos=DF|name=[[Lusajo Mwaikenda]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|10|27|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=22|pos=DF|name=[[Shomari Kapombe]]|age={{Birth date and age|1992|01|28|df=yes}}|caps=95|goals=2|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Ibrahim Ame|age={{birth date and age|1993|03|12|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=Mashujaa|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|MAR}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Miraji Abdallah|age={{birth date and age|2004|06|30|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Coastal Union F.C.|Coastal Union]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|MAR}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Abdulrazack Hamza|age={{Birth date and age|2003|03|23|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|MAR}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=17|pos=MF|name=Khalid Habibu|age={{birth date and age|2001|09|10|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Singida Black Stars FC|Singida Black Stars]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=10|pos=FW|name=[[Mbwana Samatta]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|12|23|df=yes}}|caps=89|goals=22|club=[[Le Havre AC|Le Havre]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Kibu Denis]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|12|4|df=y}}|caps=27|goals=0|club=[[Simba S.C.|Simba]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Abdul Hamisi Suleiman|age={{Birth date and age|df=yes|2001|2|26}}|caps=27|goals=4|club=[[Azam F.C.|Azam]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=[[2025 Africa Cup of Nations]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Clement Mzize]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|1|7|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[Young Africans S.C.|Young Africans]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|MAR}}, 25 March 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.<br>
<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br>
<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.<br>
<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end|background=}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|4 January 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Tanzania |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/185/Tanzania.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Tanzania.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" style="width:175px;"|Name
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!class="unsortable" style="width:110px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|[[Erasto Nyoni]]
|107
|7
|2006–2021
|-
|2
|align=left|'''[[Simon Msuva]]'''
|103
|25
|2012–present
|-
|3
|align=left|[[Mrisho Ngassa]]
|100
|25
|2006–2015
|-
|4
|align=left|[[Kelvin Yondani]]
|97
|0
|2008–2021
|-
|5
|align=left|'''[[Shomari Kapombe]]'''
|95
|2
|2011–present
|-
|6
|align=left|'''[[Mbwana Samatta]]'''
|89
|22
|2011–present
|-
|7
|align=left|[[Raphael Bocco|John Bocco]]
|84
|16
|2009–2023
|-
|8
|align=left|'''[[Himid Mao]]'''
|81
|2
|2013–present
|-
|9
|align=left|[[Juma Kaseja]]
|79
|0
|2002–2021
|-
|10
|align=left|'''[[Aishi Manula]]'''
|68
|0
|2015–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
!class="unsortable" width=175px|Name
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!class="unsortable" width=110px|Career
|-
|rowspan="2"|1
|align="left"|[[Mrisho Ngassa]]
|25
|100
|{{#expr:25/100 round 2}}
|2006–2015
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Simon Msuva]]'''
|25
|103
|{{#expr:25/103 round 2}}
|2012–present
|-
|3
|align="left"|'''[[Mbwana Samatta]]'''
|22
|89
|{{#expr:22/89 round 2}}
|2011–present
|-
|4
|align="left"|[[Raphael Bocco|John Bocco]]
|16
|84
|{{#expr:16/84 round 2}}
|2009–2023
|-
|5
|align="left"|[[John Nteze Lungu]]
|12
|22
|{{#expr:12/22 round 2}}
|1992–2002
|-
|rowspan=2|6
|align="left"|[[Jerson Tegete]]
|8
|28
|{{#expr:8/28 round 2}}
|2006–2011
|-
|align="left"|[[Danny Mrwanda]]
|8
|34
|{{#expr:8/34 round 2}}
|2005–2011
|-
|rowspan=3|8
|align="left"|[[Mohamed Rajab (footballer)|Mohamed Rajab]]
|7
|20
|{{#expr:7/20 round 2}}
|2002–2011
|-
|align="left"|[[Thomas Ulimwengu]]
|7
|51
|{{#expr:7/51 round 2}}
|2010–2021
|-
|align="left"|[[Erasto Nyoni]]
|7
|107
|{{#expr:7/107 round 2}}
|2006–2021
|-
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=30|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|Colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|Colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]] and [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|Colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|Colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|Colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|3
|0
|2
|1
|1
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|Colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|Colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4
|1
|1
|2
|7
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|2
|0
|2
|0
|1
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|rowspan=8 colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|2
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6
|2
|2
|2
|9
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|8
|3
|0
|5
|10
|14
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|4
|1
|1
|2
|4
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|8
|2
|4
|2
|8
|10
|- 
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|8
|3
|1
|4
|6
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]] 
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]] 
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/16|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!49
!12
!15
!22
!49
!67
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{{main|Tanzania at the Africa Cup of Nations}}

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
! rowspan="43" |
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
!colspan=9|as {{flagcountry|Tanganyika (1961–1964)}}
!colspan=6|as {{flagcountry|Tanganyika (1961–1964)}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Not affiliated to CAF''||colspan=6 rowspan=4|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 Africa Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
!colspan=9|as {{flag|Tanzania}}
!colspan=6|as {{flag|Tanzania}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|colspan=6|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''||colspan=6|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''||4||1||1||2||3||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]||2||0||1||1||2||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]||4||1||2||1||5||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]||2||0||1||1||3||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''||colspan=6|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 Africa Cup of Nations|1980]]||Group stage ||7th||3||0||1||2||3||6||4||2||1||1||8||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 Africa Cup of Nations|1982]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''||colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 Africa Cup of Nations|1984]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''||2||0||1||1||3||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations|1986]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''||colspan=6|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 Africa Cup of Nations|1988]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''||3||0||1||2||3||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 Africa Cup of Nations|1990]]||2||0||2||0||2||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations|1992]]||6||1||2||3||4||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations|1994]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''||colspan=6|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''||10||4||0||6||14||15
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations|1998]]||6||1||2||3||5||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]||2||0||0||2||0||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]||2||0||0||2||2||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew during qualifying''||colspan=6|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]||colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not qualify''||2||0||1||1||0||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]||6||2||2||2||4||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]||6|2||2||2||2||9||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]||6||1||2||3||6||9
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]||2||0||2||0||2||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]||4||1||2||1||6||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]||4||0||1||3||0||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]||Group stage||24th||3||0||0||3||2||8||6||2||2||2||6||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''||6||2||1||3||5||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]||Group stage||22nd||3||0||2||1||1||4||6||2||2||2||3||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]||Round of 16
|16th
|4
|0
|2
|2
|3
|5
||6||3||1||2||5||4
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]||colspan=8|''Qualified as co-hosts''||colspan=6|''Qualified as co-hosts''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
| colspan="8" |''To be determined''
| colspan="6" |''To be determined''
|-
!Total||Round of 16||4/35||13||0||5||8||9||23||99||25||32||42||100||134
|}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
!width=1% rowspan=13|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 3
!colspan=6|Appearances: 7
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||Group stage|| 5th||3||1||1||1||2||2||6||4||1||1||10||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]||colspan="8" rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''||3||1||1||1||7||2
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||2||0||0||2||1||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]||2||0||1||1||1||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]||2||0||2||0||1||1
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]||Group stage||10th||3||1||1||1||3||4||4||1||2||1||2||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]||colspan="8"|''Did not qualify''||4||2||0||2||3||5
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]||'''Quarter-finals'''
|8th
|5
|3
|1
|1
|5
|2|| colspan="6" |''Qualified as hosts''
|-
!'''Total'''
!'''Quarter-finals'''
!'''2/7'''
!'''6'''
!'''2'''
!'''2'''
!'''2'''
!'''5'''
!'''6'''
!'''23'''
!'''8'''
!'''7'''
!'''8'''
!'''25'''
!'''22'''
|}

===African Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the African Games|African Games]] record
|-
!Year||Result||Pld||W||D||L||GF||GA
|-
|{{flagicon|NGR}} [[Football at the 1973 All-Africa Games|1973]]||7th||3||0||0||3||3||7
|-
!'''Total'''||1/4||3||0||0||3||3||7
|}

===Gossage Cup / CECAFA Cup===
{{Main|CECAFA Cup}}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[CECAFA Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (3):''' [[1974 CECAFA Cup|1974]], [[1994 CECAFA Cup|1994]], [[2010 CECAFA Cup|2010]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (5): [[1973 CECAFA Cup|1973]], [[1980 CECAFA Cup|1980]], [[1981 CECAFA Cup|1981]], [[1992 CECAFA Cup|1992]], [[2002 CECAFA Cup|2002]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (4): [[1975 CECAFA Cup|1975]]{{sup|s}}, [[1979 CECAFA Cup|1979]], [[1990 CECAFA Cup|1990]], [[2008 CECAFA Cup|2008]]
* '''[[COSAFA Cup]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[2017 COSAFA Cup|2017]]

===Friendly===
* '''[[CECAFA Cup#Gossage Cup (1926–1966) and Challenge Cup (1967–1971)|Gossage Cup / Challenge Cup]] (5)''': 1949, 1950, 1951, 1964, 1965

;Notes:
* {{sup|s}} Shared titles.

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/tan/ Tanzania] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/TAN Tanzania] at [[FIFA]]

{{Tanzania national football team}}
{{Football in Tanzania}}
{{CECAFA}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Tanzania}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Tanzania National Football Team}}
[[Category:Tanzania national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
